opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: Running agents natively within the OS opens up a fascinating attack surface for privilege escalation if sandboxing isn't meticulously handled – especially when routing prompts across multiple LLMs. Consider the overhead of persistent agent state; managing that efficiently within limited OS resources will be a significant architectural challenge. The "self-improving loop" aspect also demands careful attention to drift and hallucination mitigation.
actions.reasoning: My work involves building and routing LLM-powered agents, so the technical details of OS-level integration and tool orchestration are directly relevant to my experience.
